chunks.text: Carroll earned his Bachelor of Science in Biology from Texas A&M University and his Doctor of Dental Surgery from the University of Texas at Houston Dental Branch. Dr. Carroll then moved to the Midwest, where he attended the University of Iowa, completing a rigorous two-year specialty program in the practice of Endodontics. Following the completion of his specialty training, Dr. Carroll remained at the University of Iowa Dental School as a full-time faculty member in the Department of Endodontics.

We want you to feel welcomed, comfortable, and respected when in our office. We treat our patients as unique individuals and tailor our treatment directly to your specific needs. We also pride ourselves on quality and efficient communication, both with the patient and the referring doctor, so that your transition to our office will be as easy and seamless as possible. Please explore our site, learn about our office and services, and get to know our doctor and staff better before your appointment.

If you have been referred to us for endodontic treatment, you can rest assured that your dentist desired for you to receive the highest level of clinical care from a professional and highly-trained team. At Denton Endodontics our number one goal is to provide you with quality-centered treatment aimed at assisting you in maintaining your natural teeth and healthy smile. Along with state-of-the-art treatment, you can expect warm and friendly visits with our doctor and staff at Denton Endodontics.

--- Dr. Carroll ---
Denton Texas Endodontics Denton Texas Endodontics Home Meet Us Dr. Carroll Staff Office Tour Location Procedures Root Canal Therapy Endodontic Retreatment Apicoectomy Patient Information Patient Forms Instructions Financial Policy Referring Doctors Dr. Carroll Dr. Brandon Carroll, DDS A native Texan, Dr. Brandon Carroll was born and raised in Arlington, Texas. Dr.

Carroll is also a participating member in American Association of Endodontists, the American Dental Association, the Texas Dental Association, and the Denton County Study Club. When not in the office, Dr. Carroll enjoys spending time with his family, his wife Leslie, and his son Everett. Dr. Carroll also thinks his dog June is pretty special. Dr. Carroll also enjoys outdoor photography, bass fishing, playing softball, and cheering on the DFW sports teams.

Carroll opened Denton Endodontics, focusing on what he is most passionate about in dentistry, patient care. Dr. Carroll continually strives to treat his patients with not only the highest level of clinical care possible but also with a compassionate understanding that puts his patients at ease. Dr. Carroll views each patient as an individual and recognizes that they have unique needs.

He enjoys taking the time to educate his patients about their specific condition, their treatment needs, and the overall treatment process. Dr. Carroll is a Diplomate of the American Board of Endodontics, or in other words, he is Board Certified. This is a distinction that less than 20% of currently practicing endodontists can claim. Dr. Carroll is proud to have obtained this status, as he feels that it exhibits his commitment to the profession of endodontics and to his patients. Dr.

Denton Texas Endodontics

During his tenure, Dr. Carroll taught dental students, residents specializing in endodontics, and local dentists how to perform root canal treatment using the most current and advanced techniques and materials. Dr. Carroll found his faculty position to be very rewarding and enjoyed the opportunity to pass on his knowledge and expertise to others in the dental field. Seeking a return to his home state of Texas, Dr.

--- Staff ---
Denton Texas Endodontics Denton Texas Endodontics Home Meet Us Dr. Carroll Staff Office Tour Location Procedures Root Canal Therapy Endodontic Retreatment Apicoectomy Patient Information Patient Forms Instructions Financial Policy Referring Doctors Staff The Denton Endodontics staff members are properly licensed and highly trained to assist Dr. Carroll in providing you with excellent treatment in a comfortable environment.

At Denton Endodontics we use state of the art equipment such as digital x-rays and surgical microscopes to give you the finest endodontic treatment available. We also work hard to create a relaxing and comfortable environment for our patients. Dr. Carroll has a special interest in landscape photography, and every picture hanging in the office is one of his personal photographs, so please ask Dr. Carroll about them because each one has a story.

Also with our patients in mind, each treatment room has a ceiling mounted T. V. so that as Dr. Carroll completes your treatment, you can sit back and relax in comfort. Carroll Staff Office Tour Location Procedures Root Canal Therapy Endodontic Retreatment Apicoectomy Patient Information Patient Forms Instructions Financial Policy Referring Doctors Instructions Initial Visit to Denton Endodontics Please bring the following items to your 1 st visit: Dental insurance card, if any Driver’s license or photo ID Completed patient forms, if possible – visit the Patient Forms page to print of all of the forms (would be nice to have “Patient Forms” in this line as a link to the patient forms page) Following Root Canal Therapy or Retreatment The tooth and surrounding gum tissue may be slightly sore and tender for several days due to the procedure itself and the previous condition of your tooth.

This discomfort is absolutely normal and is no cause for alarm. In most cases, this discomfort gradually tapers off after 48-72 hours. For your mild discomfort, we highly recommend taking what you would normally take for a headache. Use over-the-counter medications such as ibuprofen (Advil or Motrin), naproxen (Aleve), or acetaminophen (Tylenol) as directed on the package and by Dr. Carroll.

Should you have discomfort that is not alleviated by the above medications or experience swelling of the surrounding tissues following treatment, please contact our office for further instructions. Do not chew food on the treated tooth until all treatment is completed, including the placement of a filling or crown by your restorative dentist.

After this time period, a transition can be made to harder foods, but tough foods such as steak or crunchy food such as chips should still be avoided until days 5-7. Avoid brushing the sutured/surgical area for the first 3-5 days. Light rinses (1 tsp. salt in 8 oz. warm water) can be used to keep the area clean during this time period as well as the use of a moist Q-tip. At day 5, light brushing of the area can be resumed, with normal brushing beginning day 7-10.

--- Instructions ---

Use over-the-counter medications such as ibuprofen (Advil or Motrin), naproxen (Aleve), or acetaminophen (Tylenol) as directed on the package and by Dr. Carroll. Avoid all temptation to lift your lip and take a look at the sutures that have been placed. This tension can cause the sutures to become loose and can result in less satisfactory gum healing. Activities such as vigorous chewing can cause the same result.

If you believe that any sutures have been lost in the first 48 hours, contact our office. You may need to be seen to replace the lost suture(s). Most sutures are designed to dissolve on their own between days 5-14, so suture loss during this time frame is normal and expected. Soft foods such as puddings, Jell-O, mashed potatoes, and broth soups are recommended in the first 48 hours.

Failure to follow this instruction could result in fracture of the tooth or loss of the temporary filling, possibly leading to recontamination of the root canal system. Following Endodontic Surgery (Apicoectomy) Expect mild discomfort at the surgical site as well as bruising and minor swelling of the soft tissues. This is normal for the first several days following the procedure.

This external financing option allows you to pay over time for the treatment you need today. The CareCredit plans offered through our office are available with no interest charged to the patient. If interested in this payment option, please visit the CareCredit website to learn more and submit an application. So that your time in our office may be focused on your dental treatment needs, we ask that you would submit this application prior to your initial visit.

Once printed and filled out, the form can be faxed to us at (940) 387-4636, or emailed with digital copies of the radiographs to Denton. Endodontics@gmail. com. Although we prefer to have this information prior to the patient’s visit, the form and radiographs can also be given to the patient to bring with them to their appointment if needed.

The fee for your endodontic treatment will be based on the extent of treatment required. During your first visit we will discuss the probable number of visits, their length, and the fees involved. Endodontic fees usually range from $900 to $1,600, and it is our policy that your care is paid for at the time of treatment. Insurance Coverage Our office is committed to helping you maximize your insurance benefits. However, patients assume ultimate financial responsibility for their care.
